data.title: The U.S. job market remains stable, and the Fed may postpone the resumption of interest rate cuts until September
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. job market performed solidly in June, with non-farm payrolls increasing by 147,000, higher than the revised 144,000 in May; the unemployment rate unexpectedly fell to 4.1%, while economists had expected a slight increase to 4.3%. The report shows that the labor market remains stable, which may cause the Federal Reserve to postpone the resumption of interest rate cuts to September. Although job growth exceeded expectations, the growth rate is slowing, mainly reflecting weak hiring activities. Layoffs remain quite low, and employers generally hoarded workers during and after the new crown pandemic because they had difficulty finding labor. Several indicators, including the number of first-time unemployment claims and the number of people receiving unemployment benefits, show that the labor market is showing signs of fatigue after a strong performance that protected the economy from recession. At that time, the Federal Reserve tightened monetary policy significantly to combat high inflation. (Jinshi)

data.title: YZi Labs Invests in Digital Asset’s $135M Series E Round
data.description: YZi Labs announced on X today that it joins leading financial institutions such as DRW, Tradeweb, Goldman Sachs, DTCC, and Citadel in investing in Digital Asset’s $135M Series E round, signaling its support of institutional adoption around compliant blockchain infrastructure.The investment will fuel the growth of the Canton Network, the only public L1 with configurable privacy, enabling regulated institutions to bring trillions in real-world assets on-chain.Digital Asset is a platform for enterprise-grade tools to build compliant solutions for regulated markets and the creator of the Canton Network. By joining this round, YZi Labs reinforces its commitment to advancing institutional adoption through infrastructure that meets the regulatory and operational needs of mature markets like the U.S.As of June 2025, the Canton Network has supported tokenized real-world assets with a total value locked (TVL) of trillions of dollars, including over $1.5 trillion in monthly tokenized U.S. Treasury repo activity. The network has seen notable growth over the past year with nearly 400 participants across its ecosystem, including global leaders in both traditional and decentralized finance.

data.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 Futures Rise Following Employment Data Release
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the S&P 500 futures briefly reached an intraday high following the release of non-farm employment data. Similarly, the Nasdaq 100 futures also touched an intraday peak after the announcement.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 109,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.55% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 03, 2025, 12:40 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 109,000 USDT and is now trading at 108,959.992188 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.55% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: US non-farm data far exceeded expectations, which may ease the pressure on the Fed to cut interest rates
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. non-farm payrolls unexpectedly grew by 147,000 in June, which was not only higher than the 144,000 new jobs in May, but also significantly exceeded the 110,000 generally predicted by economists, showing that the U.S. labor market has successfully withstood the uncertainty brought about by the Trump administration's trade and immigration policies. This unexpectedly strong data will reduce the pressure on the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates, despite Trump's repeated public calls for the bank to take action to cut interest rates. Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said this week that a rate cut in July "is still under consideration," which seems to have reversed his previous position of "keeping interest rates unchanged until the fall." (Jinshi)

data.title: Rate Futures Traders Abandon July Fed Rate Cut Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, following the release of the non-farm employment report, rate futures traders have abandoned their bets on a July interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Analysts: The Fed has reasons not to cut interest rates for a longer period of time
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Analyst Jonnelle Marte said that before delving into these data, the unexpectedly strong non-farm payrolls may support the Fed's case for staying on hold for longer this year, and will almost certainly eliminate concerns about the labor market, thus ruling out the possibility of a rate cut in July. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Unemployment Rate Falls to 4.1% in June, Beating Expectations
data.description: Key Takeaways:June unemployment rate came in at 4.1%, down from 4.2% in May.The figure beat market expectations of 4.3%, signaling stronger labor market resilience.Data may influence Fed policy outlook amid ongoing inflation and rate cut discussions.The U.S. unemployment rate dropped to 4.1% in June, according to new labor market data released Friday. This marks a slight improvement from 4.2% in May and came in below expectations of 4.3%, indicating a better-than-anticipated performance in the job market.

data.title: U.S. June Employment Figures Surpass Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the United States reported an increase of 147,000 in seasonally adjusted non-farm employment for June. This figure exceeded the anticipated 110,000 and was an upward revision from the previous month's adjusted figure of 144,000.

data.title: Federal Reserve's July Rate Decision Likely to Hold Steady
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 76.7% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates in July, with a 23.3% chance of a 25 basis point cut. Looking ahead to September, there is a 4.9% probability of rates remaining unchanged, a 73.3% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point cut, and a 21.8% likelihood of a cumulative 50 basis point reduction. Recently, the U.S. ADP employment data unexpectedly showed a negative value, and the official non-farm payroll data is set to be released soon.

data.title: After the release of non-agricultural data, the market predicts that the probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in September is about 80%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: After the release of the non-farm payrolls report, interest rate futures traders gave up their bets on the Fed's rate cut in July. The market currently has an 80% chance of a Fed rate cut in September, down from 98% before the release of the non-farm payrolls report. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump moves to shut Vietnam tariff backdoors exploited by China
data.description:
The United States and Vietnam have agreed on a two-layer tariff system designed to stop goods routed through Vietnam, often to dodge US duties, from entering American markets. Under the plan, items genuinely produced in Vietnam face a 20% duty, while those judged to be merely passing through will have a 40% levy. Analysts say […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-to-close-vietnam-backdoor-tariff-china/

data.title: Bitcoin’s climb past $110k to within 2% of ATH tightens spread between USD and USDT pairs
data.description:
Bitcoin broke above $110,000 on July 3, capping a multi-day rally that perfectly illustrated the difference in how it trades across fiat and stablecoin markets. On Binance, the largest exchange by trading volume, BTCUSD held a consistent but narrow premium over BTCUSD in the days leading up to the move, averaging $36. 33, or around 0. 034%
source: https://cryptoslate.com/insights/bitcoins-climb-past-110k-to-within-2-of-ath-tightens-spread-between-usd-and-usdt-pairs/

data.title: Owlto releases product strategy upgrade to promote cross-chain stablecoin infrastructure with USD1 as the core
U.S. Employment Report to Influence Dollar's Trajectory
data.description: According to Odaily, an analyst from ING, Turner, has highlighted the significance of the upcoming U.S. non-farm employment report in determining whether the recent downward trend of the dollar will persist. U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has indicated that persistent inflation and a robust labor market suggest that interest rates should remain at restrictive levels. Turner noted that any unexpected downturn in the employment report could undermine Powell's position and increase market expectations for a rate cut at the July meeting. He added that unless the employment data is weaker than anticipated, the dollar is likely to continue consolidating ahead of the July 4th U.S. holiday.

data.title: YZi Labs announces investment in Digital Asset
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, YZi Labs announced today that it has invested in Digital Asset, an enterprise-level tool platform that builds compliance solutions for regulated markets. The investment is based on Digital Asset's $135 million Series E financing announced on June 24, with participation from major financial institutions including DRW, Tradeweb, DTCC, Citadel Securities and Goldman Sachs. The funds will enable Digital Asset to expand the Canton Network's infrastructure, absorb new institutions more quickly, and support the composability and interoperability required to bring billions of dollars of real-world assets on-chain.

data.title: Binance Introduces Upgrades to Pay Send Service for Enhanced Crypto Transfers
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has unveiled two significant upgrades to its Binance Pay Send service, aimed at streamlining the process of sending cryptocurrencies. These enhancements, Send via Contact and Send On-Chain, are designed to cater to diverse user preferences, offering improved speed, security, and convenience.

Binance Pay Send via Contact allows users to send crypto instantly to phone contacts without the need to input wallet addresses, emails, or Binance IDs. This feature supports over 300 cryptocurrencies and eliminates gas fees, providing a time-saving solution for users. By selecting a contact from their phone book within the Binance App, users can send crypto with minimal effort, reducing errors associated with manual entry of payment details. This upgrade enhances social connectivity by enabling users to connect with friends and family, making transfers between Binance users more intuitive.

The Send On-Chain feature facilitates crypto transfers by scanning a QR code or capturing a photo of a wallet address, eliminating manual input and network complexities. Utilizing AI-powered OCR technology, this feature automatically extracts payment details from images or screenshots, speeding up transactions and minimizing errors. It highlights character similarities and provides secondary confirmation for new addresses, ensuring enhanced accuracy and security. Additionally, this upgrade supports the widespread adoption of stablecoins by simplifying on-chain transactions.

Binance Pay Send is not available in certain restricted countries or regions due to legal and regulatory requirements. Users are advised to check the availability of these services in their region. The announcement emphasizes that discrepancies may exist between the original English content and translated versions, urging users to refer to the English version for the most accurate information.

data.title: U.S. Stablecoin Policy May Disrupt Global Payment Systems, Says AMUNDI
data.description: According to PANews, AMUNDI, Europe's largest asset management firm, has expressed concerns that the United States' stablecoin policy could potentially disrupt the global payment systems. This statement was reported by CGTN America. AMUNDI's analysis highlights the significant impact that U.S. regulatory decisions on stablecoins might have on international financial transactions and stability.

data.title: Robinhood CEO: OpenAI and SpaceX tokenized stocks are derivatives, not equity
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev said, "Tokenized stock promotion is the seed of a larger vision. These tokens are not technically equivalent to stocks, but they still allow retail investors to track changes in company valuations. Users can see the specific mechanism in our terms." The brokerage's official website also clearly pointed out that these "stock tokens" are not real company equity, but a blockchain-based derivative contract whose price tracks the market value changes of real stocks. 
As previously reported, OpenAI officially issued a statement denying that it authorized Robinhood to launch related tokenized stocks. The so-called "OpenAI token" is not the company's equity, nor has it established any partnership with Robinhood or participated in endorsement-related projects. OpenAI emphasized that any transfer involving the company's equity must be officially approved, and the company has not yet approved any such transactions, and called on the public to remain vigilant. (The Block)

data.title: JPMorgan Chase expects the stablecoin market to reach $500 billion in 2028, far below optimistic forecasts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News JPMorgan Chase released a research report predicting that the global stablecoin market will grow to US$500 billion in 2028, far lower than the US$1 trillion to US$2 trillion predicted by some institutions. The bank pointed out that 88% of the current demand for stablecoins comes from crypto-native activities (such as trading, DeFi mortgages), and only 6% is used in payment scenarios. The report shows that stablecoins are difficult to replace bank deposits or money funds on a large scale, mainly due to lack of returns and friction in fiat-crypto conversion. Analysts refuted the view that stablecoins are comparable to China's digital RMB or mobile payments, emphasizing the essential differences between the two in centralized models. It is worth noting that Standard Chartered Bank predicted in April that if the US GENIUS Act is passed, the supply of stablecoins may surge to US$2 trillion in 2028. JPMorgan Chase believes that stablecoins are more likely to maintain a moderate growth path led by crypto demand rather than achieve widespread payment applications. (CoinDesk)
